sentences.quote: We formalize the verdict that electroweak baryogenesis is forbidden in the Standard Model under \emph{two independent} obstructions: \textit{(i)} the chirality-wall obstruction at the gauging step, and \textit{(ii)} the crossover nature of the SM electroweak phase transition.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize the verdict that electroweak baryogenesis is forbidden in the standard model under two independent obstructions: (i) the chirality-wall obstruction at the gauging step, and (ii) the crossover nature of the sm electroweak phase transition.
sentences.agent_notes: Abstract setup; metaclaim summarising the paper's two obstructions.

sentences.quote: The first reflects the Wang $\Zsixteen$ anomaly classification~\cite{WangWanYou2022}: the SM-no-$\nu_R$ fermion content gives $3 \times 15 = 45 \equiv 13 \not\equiv 0 \pmod{16}$, blocking non-perturbative lattice gauging~\cite{Wang2020} and depriving the sphaleron rate of a first-principles regulator.
sentences.quote_normalized: the first reflects the wang $\zsixteen$ anomaly classification~: the sm-no-$\nu_r$ fermion content gives $3 \times 15 = 45 \equiv 13 \not\equiv 0 \pmod{16}$, blocking non-perturbative lattice gauging~ and depriving the sphaleron rate of a first-principles regulator.
sentences.agent_notes: IA arithmetic: 3×15=45 ✓; 45 mod 16 = 13 ✓. Both verified. However Wang2020 bibitem in paper text refers to 'Nucl. Phys. B 980 115798 (2022); arXiv:2008.06499' but CITATION_REGISTRY['Wang2020'] resolves to a DIFFERENT paper (Phys. Rev. Research 2 013189 (2020), arXiv:1910.14664) — BIBKEY COLLISION / wrong-target citation. FAIL.

sentences.quote: The second is a lattice result: the resummed-thermal-corrections endpoint at $m_H = 72.4 \pm 1.7$~GeV (Csikor, Fodor, and Heitger~\cite{CsikorFodorHeitger1999}, refining Kajantie--Laine--Rummukainen--Shaposhnikov~\cite{KLRS1996}) sits well below the PDG value $m_H = 125.20$~GeV~\cite{PDG2024}; the SM electroweak transition is a crossover, blocking sphaleron decoupling (Sakharov's third condition).
sentences.quote_normalized: the second is a lattice result: the resummed-thermal-corrections endpoint at $m_h = 72.4 \pm 1.7$~gev (csikor, fodor, and heitger~, refining kajantie--laine--rummukainen--shaposhnikov~) sits well below the pdg value $m_h = 125.20$~gev~; the sm electroweak transition is a crossover, blocking sphaleron decoupling (sakharov's third condition).
sentences.agent_notes: KLRS endpoint 72.4±1.7 GeV matches EWBG_PARAMS.KLRS_M_H_CROSSOVER_THRESHOLD_GEV=72.4 and *_UNCERTAINTY_GEV=1.7. SM m_H=125.20 GeV matches EWBG_PARAMS.SM_M_H_GEV=125.2. All three bibitems resolve in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: We ship \texttt{EWBaryogenesisChiralityWall.lean}: $16$ theorems formalizing the sphaleron-suppression structural form, the chirality-wall predicate \WallIntact{} / \WallCracked{} over $\Zsixteen$, substantive cross-bridges to \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.three\_gen\_anomalous} and \texttt{sm\_anomaly\_with\_nu\_R}, the compound \EWBGViable{} predicate, the load-bearing biconditional $\neg \EWBGViable{} \leftrightarrow (\WallIntact \vee \neg \mathsf{IsBaryogenesisViable})$, and the punchline \texttt{sm\_no\_nu\_R\_ewbg\_doubly\_forbidden}: under the KLRS-crossover hypothesis, the SM-as-is fails \emph{both} conditions simultaneously.
sentences.quote_normalized: we ship ewbaryogenesischiralitywall.lean: $16$ theorems formalizing the sphaleron-suppression structural form, the chirality-wall predicate \wallintact{} / \wallcracked{} over $\zsixteen$, substantive cross-bridges to z16anomalycomputation.three\_gen\_anomalous and sm\_anomaly\_with\_nu\_r, the compound \ewbgviable{} predicate, the load-bearing biconditional $\neg \ewbgviable{} \leftrightarrow (\wallintact \vee \neg \mathsf{isbaryogenesisviable})$, and the punchline sm\_no\_nu\_r\_ewbg\_doubly\_forbidden: under the klrs-crossover hypothesis, the sm-as-is fails both conditions simultaneously.
sentences.agent_notes: IA count check: paper says '16 theorems' in EWBaryogenesisChiralityWall.lean; current `grep -c '^theorem ' EWBaryogenesisChiralityWall.lean` = 16 ✓. Z16AnomalyComputation.three_gen_anomalous and .sm_anomaly_with_nu_R use project paper-convention <Module>.<symbol> — actual ship is in module `Z16AnomalyComputation` with full name `SKEFTHawking.three_gen_anomalous`. Per Phase 6i Wave 4 audit, this idiom is accepted (DRIFTED-but-resolvable). All other theorem refs resolve in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: The key insight is that the two failure modes are \emph{independent}: SM-no-$\nu_R$ fails the wall regardless of the transition; SM with three right-handed neutrinos cracks the wall but fails the transition.
sentences.quote_normalized: the key insight is that the two failure modes are independent: sm-no-$\nu_r$ fails the wall regardless of the transition; sm with three right-handed neutrinos cracks the wall but fails the transition.
sentences.agent_notes: Backed by both Lean theorems; PASS.

sentences.quote: Either failure suffices to forbid SM EWBG.
sentences.quote_normalized: either failure suffices to forbid sm ewbg.
sentences.agent_notes: Restatement of doubly-forbidden punchline.

sentences.quote: The formalization invokes Phase 5z.3 \texttt{crossover\_excludes\_baryogenesis} and Phase 5e \texttt{three\_gen\_anomalous} in proof bodies; it is zero-sorry, introduces zero new axioms, and is consistent with the existing project axiom budget.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization invokes phase 5z.3 crossover\_excludes\_baryogenesis and phase 5e three\_gen\_anomalous in proof bodies; it is zero-sorry, introduces zero new axioms, and is consistent with the existing project axiom budget.
sentences.agent_notes: Both theorems exist; module has 0 sorry / 0 new axioms (verified by lake build). Standard project axiom budget.

sentences.quote: The Standard Model has two well-known obstructions to electroweak baryogenesis (EWBG).
sentences.quote_normalized: the standard model has two well-known obstructions to electroweak baryogenesis (ewbg).

sentences.quote: The first is dynamical: insufficient CP violation in the Cabibbo--Kobayashi--Maskawa phase, by approximately ten orders of magnitude relative to what the observed baryon asymmetry requires~\cite{Gavela1994,Huet1994}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the first is dynamical: insufficient cp violation in the cabibbo--kobayashi--maskawa phase, by approximately ten orders of magnitude relative to what the observed baryon asymmetry requires~.
sentences.agent_notes: Both citations resolve in CITATION_REGISTRY with primary-source cache.

sentences.quote: The second is thermodynamic: the electroweak phase transition is a crossover for the physical Higgs mass~\cite{KLRS1996,CsikorFodorHeitger1999}, leaving Sakharov's third condition (departure from thermal equilibrium) unrealized.
sentences.quote_normalized: the second is thermodynamic: the electroweak phase transition is a crossover for the physical higgs mass~, leaving sakharov's third condition (departure from thermal equilibrium) unrealized.

sentences.quote: We formalize these results and add a third obstruction --- the \emph{chirality-wall obstruction} from non-perturbative $\Zsixteen$-anomaly classification~\cite{WangWanYou2022,Wang2020} --- inside a single Lean module that bridges the chirality-wall pillar (Phases 5a, 5e, 5p) and the phase-transition pillar (Phase 5z.3).
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize these results and add a third obstruction --- the chirality-wall obstruction from non-perturbative $\zsixteen$-anomaly classification~ --- inside a single lean module that bridges the chirality-wall pillar (phases 5a, 5e, 5p) and the phase-transition pillar (phase 5z.3).
sentences.agent_notes: Re-uses Wang2020 bibkey — same wrong-target collision as in abstract. Bibkey resolves to PRR 2 013189 (arXiv 1910.14664) but bibitem text claims NPB 980 115798 / arXiv 2008.06499. FAIL.

sentences.quote: The two obstructions our paper formalizes act independently.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two obstructions our paper formalizes act independently.

sentences.quote: The SM-no-$\nu_R$ fermion content carries a $\Zsixteen$ anomaly of $13$ mod $16$ (= $-3$); cancellation is required for the gauging step (Phase 5p \texttt{gauging\_requires\_z16\_cancellation}), so the SM-as-is is non-perturbatively obstructed before the question of transition order even applies.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sm-no-$\nu_r$ fermion content carries a $\zsixteen$ anomaly of $13$ mod $16$ (= $-3$); cancellation is required for the gauging step (phase 5p gauging\_requires\_z16\_cancellation), so the sm-as-is is non-perturbatively obstructed before the question of transition order even applies.
sentences.agent_notes: 13 mod 16 = -3 is verified by SKEFTHawking.three_gen_is_neg3 and matches EWBG_PARAMS.SM_Z16_ANOMALY_NO_NU_R=13. GaugingStep.gauging_requires_z16_cancellation = SKEFTHawking.gauging_requires_z16_cancellation (paper-convention <Module>.<symbol> — accepted idiom).

sentences.quote: Adding three right-handed neutrinos brings the anomaly to $48 \equiv 0$ mod $16$, cracking the wall, but under the KLRS hypothesis the resummed transition is still a crossover and the exclusion stands.
sentences.quote_normalized: adding three right-handed neutrinos brings the anomaly to $48 \equiv 0$ mod $16$, cracking the wall, but under the klrs hypothesis the resummed transition is still a crossover and the exclusion stands.
sentences.agent_notes: 48 mod 16 = 0 verified; SM_Z16_ANOMALY_WITH_3NU_R=0 in EWBG_PARAMS. sm_with_3nu_R_wall_cracks present in module.

sentences.quote: Our load-bearing theorem is the biconditional
sentences.quote_normalized: our load-bearing theorem is the biconditional

sentences.quote: \begin{equation}\label{eq:biconditional}\neg \EWBGViable{}(a, p, c) \;\;\Leftrightarrow\;\; \bigl( \WallIntact{}(a) \;\vee\; \neg\, \mathsf{IsBaryogenesisViable}(p, c) \bigr),\end{equation}
sentences.quote_normalized: \begin{equation}\neg \ewbgviable{}(a, p, c) \;\;\leftrightarrow\;\; \bigl( \wallintact{}(a) \;\vee\; \neg\, \mathsf{isbaryogenesisviable}(p, c) \bigr),\end{equation}
sentences.agent_notes: Eq. (1) is the load-bearing biconditional; theorem ships in module.

sentences.quote: where $a \in \Zsixteen$ is the fermion-content anomaly, $p \in \mathsf{EWFiniteTParams}$ encodes the finite-$T$ effective potential parameters, and $c \in \mathbb{R}$ is the Cohen--Kaplan--Nelson sphaleron-decoupling threshold~\cite{CohenKaplanNelson1993}.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $a \in \zsixteen$ is the fermion-content anomaly, $p \in \mathsf{ewfinitetparams}$ encodes the finite-$t$ effective potential parameters, and $c \in \mathbb{r}$ is the cohen--kaplan--nelson sphaleron-decoupling threshold~.
sentences.agent_notes: EWFiniteTParams resolves; CohenKaplanNelson1993 in registry with primary-source cache.

sentences.quote: The proof is a de Morgan unfolding with case analysis on $a = 0$ and is substantive: the LHS-to-RHS direction requires constructive case splits (mere classical de Morgan would not suffice in the dependent-type setting).
sentences.quote_normalized: the proof is a de morgan unfolding with case analysis on $a = 0$ and is substantive: the lhs-to-rhs direction requires constructive case splits (mere classical de morgan would not suffice in the dependent-type setting).
sentences.agent_notes: Proof body in module uses by_cases on anomaly = 0 — confirmed.

sentences.quote: The corollary \texttt{sm\_no\_nu\_R\_ewbg\_doubly\_forbidden} packages the two independent failure modes for SM-as-is (intact wall) and SM-with-$3\nu_R$ (crossover under KLRS) as a conjunction; this is the formal statement of ``SM EWBG is doubly forbidden.''
sentences.quote_normalized: the corollary sm\_no\_nu\_r\_ewbg\_doubly\_forbidden packages the two independent failure modes for sm-as-is (intact wall) and sm-with-$3\nu_r$ (crossover under klrs) as a conjunction; this is the formal statement of ``sm ewbg is doubly forbidden.''

sentences.quote: The remainder of this paper is organized as follows.
sentences.quote_normalized: the remainder of this paper is organized as follows.

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:sphaleron} introduces the sphaleron-suppression structural form and the Cohen--Kaplan--Nelson decoupling predicate.
sentences.quote_normalized: section introduces the sphaleron-suppression structural form and the cohen--kaplan--nelson decoupling predicate.

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:wall} formalizes the chirality-wall predicate and its substantive cross-bridges to \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation}.
sentences.quote_normalized: section formalizes the chirality-wall predicate and its substantive cross-bridges to z16anomalycomputation.
sentences.agent_notes: Section roadmap reference; Z16AnomalyComputation is a project Lean module — ls lean/SKEFTHawking/Z16AnomalyComputation.lean exists.

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:bridge} states the compound \EWBGViable{} and the load-bearing biconditional \eqref{eq:biconditional}.
sentences.quote_normalized: section states the compound \ewbgviable{} and the load-bearing biconditional .

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:sm-verdict} applies the biconditional to the SM with and without right-handed neutrinos under the KLRS hypothesis and concludes with the doubly-forbidden punchline.
sentences.quote_normalized: section applies the biconditional to the sm with and without right-handed neutrinos under the klrs hypothesis and concludes with the doubly-forbidden punchline.

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:formal-verification} surveys the formalization.
sentences.quote_normalized: section surveys the formalization.

sentences.quote: Section \ref{sec:outlook} dispatches baryogenesis to leptogenesis or BSM mechanisms.
sentences.quote_normalized: section dispatches baryogenesis to leptogenesis or bsm mechanisms.

sentences.quote: We model the dimensionless Boltzmann suppression of the sphaleron rate in the broken phase as \begin{equation}\label{eq:sphaleron-suppression}\Sigma(v, T) = e^{-v/T},\end{equation} the load-bearing structural form embedded in the Klinkhamer--Manton sphaleron rate $\Gamma_\mathrm{sphal} \sim (\alpha_W T)^4 e^{-E_\mathrm{sph}/T}$ with sphaleron energy $E_\mathrm{sph} \sim 4\pi v / g_W$.
sentences.quote_normalized: we model the dimensionless boltzmann suppression of the sphaleron rate in the broken phase as \begin{equation}\sigma(v, t) = e^{-v/t},\end{equation} the load-bearing structural form embedded in the klinkhamer--manton sphaleron rate $\gamma_sphal \sim (\alpha_w t)^4 e^{-e_sph/t}$ with sphaleron energy $e_sph \sim 4\pi v / g_w$.
sentences.agent_notes: Σ(v,T)=e^{-v/T} matches sphaleronSuppression def in Lean and sphaleron_suppression in formulas.py.

sentences.quote: The Lean theorem \texttt{sphaleronSuppression\_pos} establishes $\Sigma(v, T) > 0$ at any finite $(v, T)$, and \texttt{sphaleronSuppression\_le\_one} gives the broken-phase bound $\Sigma \le 1$ when $v/T \ge 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean theorem sphaleronsuppression\_pos establishes $\sigma(v, t) > 0$ at any finite $(v, t)$, and sphaleronsuppression\_le\_one gives the broken-phase bound $\sigma \le 1$ when $v/t \ge 0$.

sentences.quote: Sphaleron decoupling is the predicate \texttt{SphaleronsDecoupled} encoding $v(T_c)/T_c > c$ with $c = 1$ (Cohen--Kaplan--Nelson~\cite{CohenKaplanNelson1993}).
sentences.quote_normalized: sphaleron decoupling is the predicate sphaleronsdecoupled encoding $v(t_c)/t_c > c$ with $c = 1$ (cohen--kaplan--nelson~).
sentences.agent_notes: SPHALERON_DECOUPLING_THRESHOLD=1.0 matches paper's c=1.

sentences.quote: The threshold is identical to the strong-first-order condition $E/(\lambda T_c) > c$ used by \texttt{EWPhaseTransition.IsBaryogenesisViable} (Phase 5z.3).
sentences.quote_normalized: the threshold is identical to the strong-first-order condition $e/(\lambda t_c) > c$ used by ewphasetransition.isbaryogenesisviable (phase 5z.3).

sentences.quote: The chirality wall is a non-perturbative obstruction at the gauging step: lattice formulations of chiral gauge theory require $\Zsixteen$-anomaly cancellation~\cite{WangWanYou2022,Wang2020}, an anomaly-matching constraint formalized as \texttt{GaugingStep.gauging\_requires\_z16\_cancellation} in Phase 5p.
sentences.quote_normalized: the chirality wall is a non-perturbative obstruction at the gauging step: lattice formulations of chiral gauge theory require $\zsixteen$-anomaly cancellation~, an anomaly-matching constraint formalized as gaugingstep.gauging\_requires\_z16\_cancellation in phase 5p.
sentences.agent_notes: Theorem resolves under paper-convention idiom (DRIFTED but accepted). However Wang2020 bibitem still wrong-target. FAIL on citation.

sentences.quote: We define
sentences.quote_normalized: we define

sentences.quote: \begin{align}\label{eq:wall-intact}\WallIntact{}(a) &\equiv (a \ne 0 \in \Zsixteen), \\ \label{eq:wall-cracked}\WallCracked{}(a) &\equiv (a = 0 \in \Zsixteen).\end{align}
sentences.quote_normalized: \begin{align}\wallintact{}(a) &\equiv (a \ne 0 \in \zsixteen), \\ \wallcracked{}(a) &\equiv (a = 0 \in \zsixteen).\end{align}
sentences.agent_notes: Definitions match Lean: `WallIntact a := a ≠ 0` and `WallCracked a := a = 0`.

sentences.quote: \texttt{wall\_intact\_xor\_cracked} and \texttt{wall\_intact\_or\_cracked} establish disjointness and exhaustivity over $\Zsixteen$.
sentences.quote_normalized: wall\_intact\_xor\_cracked and wall\_intact\_or\_cracked establish disjointness and exhaustivity over $\zsixteen$.

sentences.quote: The substantive cross-bridges to \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation} identify the wall predicate with the project's existing $\Zsixteen$ classification:
sentences.quote_normalized: the substantive cross-bridges to z16anomalycomputation identify the wall predicate with the project's existing $\zsixteen$ classification:

sentences.quote: \texttt{sm\_no\_nu\_R\_wall\_intact} consumes \texttt{three\_gen\_anomalous} ($45 \ne 0 \in \Zsixteen$) directly to produce the wall-intact verdict for the SM-no-$\nu_R$ fermion content (3 generations $\times$ 15 components per generation, per \texttt{SMFermionData.total\_components\_without\_nu\_R}).
sentences.quote_normalized: sm\_no\_nu\_r\_wall\_intact consumes three\_gen\_anomalous ($45 \ne 0 \in \zsixteen$) directly to produce the wall-intact verdict for the sm-no-$\nu_r$ fermion content (3 generations $\times$ 15 components per generation, per smfermiondata.total\_components\_without\_nu\_r).
sentences.agent_notes: All three theorems resolve. SMFermionData.total_components_without_nu_R follows the paper-convention idiom (decl is in module SMFermionData with full name SKEFTHawking.total_components_without_nu_R). 3×15=45 ✓.

sentences.quote: \texttt{sm\_no\_nu\_R\_anomaly\_eq\_neg\_three} (substantive cross-bridge) composes the natural-representative identity $45 \equiv 13 \pmod{16}$ with the upstream \texttt{three\_gen\_is\_neg3} (Phase 5e) to identify the SM-no-$\nu_R$ $\Zsixteen$ anomaly with the canonical $-3$ form used in Wang's classification.
sentences.quote_normalized: sm\_no\_nu\_r\_anomaly\_eq\_neg\_three (substantive cross-bridge) composes the natural-representative identity $45 \equiv 13 \pmod{16}$ with the upstream three\_gen\_is\_neg3 (phase 5e) to identify the sm-no-$\nu_r$ $\zsixteen$ anomaly with the canonical $-3$ form used in wang's classification.
sentences.agent_notes: 45 ≡ 13 ≡ -3 (mod 16) verified. Both theorems resolve.

sentences.quote: \texttt{sm\_single\_gen\_with\_nu\_R\_wall\_cracks} consumes \texttt{sm\_anomaly\_with\_nu\_R} ($16 \equiv 0 \in \Zsixteen$) to produce the cracked-wall verdict at the single-generation right-handed-neutrino-completed fermion content (16 components per generation).
sentences.quote_normalized: sm\_single\_gen\_with\_nu\_r\_wall\_cracks consumes sm\_anomaly\_with\_nu\_r ($16 \equiv 0 \in \zsixteen$) to produce the cracked-wall verdict at the single-generation right-handed-neutrino-completed fermion content (16 components per generation).

sentences.quote_normalized: sm\_with\_3nu\_r\_wall\_cracks extends to three generations: $48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$.

sentences.quote: The substantive content is the \emph{identification} of the chirality-wall obstruction with the $\Zsixteen$-anomaly nonzero condition --- not merely the modular arithmetic.
sentences.quote_normalized: the substantive content is the identification of the chirality-wall obstruction with the $\zsixteen$-anomaly nonzero condition --- not merely the modular arithmetic.

sentences.quote: The compound \EWBGViable{} predicate \begin{equation}\label{eq:viability}\EWBGViable{}(a, p, c) \;\equiv\; \WallCracked{}(a) \wedge \mathsf{IsBaryogenesisViable}(p, c)\end{equation} expresses the two-pillar EWBG necessary condition: cracked wall \emph{and} sphaleron-decoupling-strength first-order transition.
sentences.quote_normalized: the compound \ewbgviable{} predicate \begin{equation}\ewbgviable{}(a, p, c) \;\equiv\; \wallcracked{}(a) \wedge \mathsf{isbaryogenesisviable}(p, c)\end{equation} expresses the two-pillar ewbg necessary condition: cracked wall and sphaleron-decoupling-strength first-order transition.
sentences.agent_notes: EWBGViable definition matches Lean exactly.

sentences.quote: The elementary bridge theorems are
sentences.quote_normalized: the elementary bridge theorems are

sentences.quote_normalized: ewbg\_forbidden\_if\_wall\_intact: a non-zero $\zsixteen$ anomaly forbids ewbg independent of transition order.

sentences.quote_normalized: ewbg\_forbidden\_if\_transition\_crossover: a crossover transition forbids ewbg independent of wall integrity. substantive cross-bridge --- the proof body invokes ewphasetransition.crossover\_excludes\_baryogenesis from phase 5z.3.

sentences.quote: The load-bearing correctness-push is the biconditional \eqref{eq:biconditional}, formalized as \texttt{ewbg\_forbidden\_iff\_wall\_intact\_or\_not\_viable}: every breakdown of EWBG must be attributed to either the wall or the phase transition, with no third failure mode in this two-pillar decomposition.
sentences.quote_normalized: the load-bearing correctness-push is the biconditional , formalized as ewbg\_forbidden\_iff\_wall\_intact\_or\_not\_viable: every breakdown of ewbg must be attributed to either the wall or the phase transition, with no third failure mode in this two-pillar decomposition.

sentences.quote: The proof is non-trivial: it requires a constructive case split on $a = 0$ (decidable on $\Zsixteen$).
sentences.quote_normalized: the proof is non-trivial: it requires a constructive case split on $a = 0$ (decidable on $\zsixteen$).

sentences.quote: The KLRS lattice analysis~\cite{KLRS1996,CsikorFodorHeitger1999} establishes that, after thermal-loop resummation, the SM electroweak transition is a crossover for $m_H > 72.4 \pm 1.7$~GeV.

sentences.quote: The two conjuncts are \emph{not} P2 bundle-redundant: they are statements about \emph{different} fermion contents (45 vs 48 mod 16) blocked by \emph{different} mechanisms (wall vs transition).

sentences.quote: A Python companion \texttt{src/ew\_baryogenesis/} (3 modules, 23-test pytest suite, $100\%$ pass) wraps the canonical formulas \texttt{sphaleron\_suppression}, \texttt{chirality\_wall\_blocks\_ewbg}, and \texttt{ewbg\_viable} (in \texttt{src/core/formulas.py}) plus the structured \texttt{SMEWBGVerdict} dataclass; the Lean and Python predicate evaluations agree across all SM benchmarks.

sentences.quote: EW baryogenesis is doubly forbidden in the SM: by the chirality-wall obstruction without right-handed neutrinos, and by the crossover nature of the resummed-thermal SM electroweak transition with or without right-handed neutrinos.
sentences.quote_normalized: ew baryogenesis is doubly forbidden in the sm: by the chirality-wall obstruction without right-handed neutrinos, and by the crossover nature of the resummed-thermal sm electroweak transition with or without right-handed neutrinos.
sentences.agent_notes: Conclusion — backed by punchline theorem.

sentences.quote: The dispatch is to leptogenesis or BSM electroweak Higgs sectors with extra scalars producing a strong first-order transition.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dispatch is to leptogenesis or bsm electroweak higgs sectors with extra scalars producing a strong first-order transition.
sentences.agent_notes: Forward-looking dispatch; INFO.

sentences.quote: For leptogenesis, the natural connection is the Phase 5z Wave 2 \texttt{MajoranaRung.lean} sterile-neutrino seesaw: with $M_R \sim 10^{14}$~GeV from the substrate-anchored hypothesis \texttt{H\_MR\_FromADWSubstrate\_BCS\_LNV} (constrained by Antusch \textit{et al.}~\cite{Antusch2003} lepton-number-symmetry obstruction), out-of-equilibrium decays of right-handed neutrinos generate a lepton asymmetry that is converted to baryon asymmetry by electroweak sphalerons \emph{above} the EW phase transition (where sphalerons are unsuppressed).
sentences.quote_normalized: for leptogenesis, the natural connection is the phase 5z wave 2 majoranarung.lean sterile-neutrino seesaw: with $m_r \sim 10^{14}$~gev from the substrate-anchored hypothesis h\_mr\_fromadwsubstrate\_bcs\_lnv (constrained by antusch et al.~ lepton-number-symmetry obstruction), out-of-equilibrium decays of right-handed neutrinos generate a lepton asymmetry that is converted to baryon asymmetry by electroweak sphalerons above the ew phase transition (where sphalerons are unsuppressed).
sentences.agent_notes: Both decl and citation resolve.

sentences.quote: The crossover transition is then no longer a problem; the wall is cracked by the $\nu_R$ completion; leptogenesis succeeds at high $T$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the crossover transition is then no longer a problem; the wall is cracked by the $\nu_r$ completion; leptogenesis succeeds at high $t$.
sentences.agent_notes: Forward-looking implication.

sentences.quote: For BSM EWBG, the requirement is the cracked-wall + first-order-strong quadrant of Fig.~\ref{fig:ewbg-allowed-region}.
sentences.quote_normalized: for bsm ewbg, the requirement is the cracked-wall + first-order-strong quadrant of fig.~.

sentences.quote: Two-Higgs-doublet models, MSSM (with light stops), or extended-scalar singlets can produce a strong first-order transition by raising the cubic coefficient at the physical $m_H$~\cite{ProfumoRamsey2007}.
sentences.quote_normalized: two-higgs-doublet models, mssm (with light stops), or extended-scalar singlets can produce a strong first-order transition by raising the cubic coefficient at the physical $m_h$~.

sentences.quote: The Lean formalization of this paper is robust to the specific BSM mechanism: the biconditional \eqref{eq:biconditional} continues to hold, the wall must crack (anomaly cancellation extends naturally to BSM with vector-like additions), and the transition order is the load-bearing input.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean formalization of this paper is robust to the specific bsm mechanism: the biconditional continues to hold, the wall must crack (anomaly cancellation extends naturally to bsm with vector-like additions), and the transition order is the load-bearing input.
sentences.agent_notes: Robustness narrative; interpretive INFO.

sentences.quote_normalized: phase 6e (nonlinear stress-energy) inherits the verdict and may need to choose between leptogenesis and bsm ewbg as the program's preferred baryogenesis mechanism.
